name: Bramble

spec.method_detail: Shake gin, lemon, and syrup with ice. Strain into rocks glass over crushed ice. Drizzle crème de mûre over the top — it bleeds through the ice.

history: Dick Bradsell created the Bramble at Fred's Club in London in 1984. He wanted to create a quintessentially British cocktail and thought of blackberries he picked as a child on the Isle of Wight. It's a relatively modern classic — less than 40 years old — but it's been adopted worldwide. The visual of the blackberry liqueur bleeding through crushed ice is part of its charm.
